_comment: ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════  TONY'S AUTONOMOUS-MODE CONFIG — tradeintel-backend  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════  Every future Claude Code session on this project MUST respect  this file. It defines:    • ALLOW — routine safe ops that auto-approve (no phone prompts)    • DENY  — dangerous ops that are blocked outright    • ASK   — medium-risk ops that fire a phone notification    • HOOKS — automatic syntax-checking of JS on edit + before commit  Crown-jewel files (index.html, admin.html, login.html) are protected  by the deny list. Edits to them require the user to temporarily move  them off the deny list — intentional friction.  Production-writing surfaces (Stripe / Cloudflare / Supabase / Railway  mutating API calls, npm install, master pushes) all prompt.  See CLAUDE.md -> Permanent Rules -> Modular architecture for context.  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // ""'); case "$f" in *.js) node --check "$f" 2>&1 || echo "[hook] SYNTAX ERROR in $f" >&2 ;; esac; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); case "$cmd" in "git commit"*|*" git commit"*) cd /home/user/tradeintel-backend && files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM -- '*.js' 2>/dev/null); for f in $files; do node --check "$f" || { echo "[pre-commit hook] syntax error in $f — blocking commit" >&2; exit 2; }; done ;; esac; exit 0
